name: George Mraz
abstract: George Mraz (born Jiří Mráz; 9 September 1944 – 16 September 2021) was a Czech-born American jazz bassist and alto saxophonist. He was a member of Oscar Peterson's group, and worked with Pepper Adams, Stan Getz, Michel Petrucciani, Stephane Grappelli, Tommy Flanagan, Jimmy Raney, Chet Baker, Barry Harris, Joe Henderson, John Abercrombie,…

about_quotes.speaker: John Abercrombie
about_quotes.evidence: me, him and George Mraz. It was like two Czechs and whatever I was... But we shared an apartment together so I knew them just as friends and roommates.
about_quotes.doc_title: John Abercrombie interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, April 19, 2001

about_quotes.speaker: Marian McPartland
about_quotes.evidence: So the Marian McPartland Dream Band, let’s recap here, will be Roy Haynes on drums. George Mraz on bass. Of course Marian on the piano.
about_quotes.doc_title: Marian McPartland — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Kenny Barron
about_quotes.evidence: I love playing with George, George Mraz. ... Since that time I’ve always loved George’s playing. ... you and George and Ben.
about_quotes.doc_title: Kenny Barron — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Harry Allen
about_quotes.evidence: I recorded one just about a month before that with Kenny Barron and Al Foster and George Mraz.
about_quotes.doc_title: Harry Allen interviewed by Monk Rowe, Chautauqua, New York, September 13, 1997

about_quotes.speaker: Randy Brecker
about_quotes.evidence: Miroslav and Mraz came to town shortly thereafter, as did Jan, we played together.
about_quotes.doc_title: Randy Brecker interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, December 12, 2022

about_quotes.speaker: Howard Alden
about_quotes.evidence: Roland Hanna and Louie Bellson is going to make it, and George Mraz, and Diz is going to come by for a few tunes... most of which he’d just written before the date.
about_quotes.doc_title: Howard Alden interviewed by Monk Rowe, Aspen, Colorado, October 11, 1997
